safePointPackageReplacementLoops.summary: Host-owned safe-point package replacement and unload execution for preloaded runtime packages. Unload clears the active package and commits an empty catalog only at a safe point, discarding pending replacement state when an active package exists. Both paths report deterministic generation and stale-handle invalidation counters without executing async streaming, eviction, renderer/RHI teardown, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, or renderer quality claims.

orderedSteps.notes: Only a successfully loaded RuntimeAssetPackage may enter pending state; failed package loads leave the active package untouched.

Duplicate or invalid package records keep the existing active package/catalog live and clear the pending package.

Clear the active package and replace the catalog with an empty generation only when an active package exists; pending replacements are discarded on successful unload and preserved when no active package exists.

hostGates.notes: Strict clang-tidy compile database availability is ready for the default dev validation path. tools/build.ps1 prepares CMake File API codemodel data before the dev configure/build, tools/validate.ps1 reuses that reply for the check-tidy.ps1 -MaxFiles 1 smoke and then runs test.ps1 -SkipBuild, while standalone tools/check-tidy.ps1 still configures the preset when native compile_commands.json is unavailable. tools/build.ps1 and tools/test.ps1 default to automatic CMake/CTest parallelism through official CMake/CTest --parallel options and accept -Jobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit lower throttle; time-sensitive host-resource tests use CTest RUN_SERIAL rather than disabling suite-wide parallelism.
